sections.9.1: Not applicable. The APS Markdown Conversion Service is an ephemeral document conversion utility that does not make, automate, or materially influence administrative actions or decisions that have legal or significant effects on individuals, groups, or communities. It functions solely as a formatting tool for public servants to convert diverse web and document formats into standardized Markdown, and does not perform any decision-making or profiling functions.
sections.9.2: Not applicable. Because the system does not perform, automate, or materially influence administrative actions affecting rights or interests (as detailed in section 9.1), there are no administrative actions of this nature to challenge, rendering a challenge process not applicable.
sections.10.2: No. No accessibility or human rights reviews have been conducted to ensure that the Markdown outputs comply with human rights obligations, such as those under the Disability Discrimination Act 1992 (Cth) (HTI State of AI Governance, p. 48, 68). Although the system is a deterministic parser, the threshold assessment notes potential risks of stripping accessibility features (such as alt text or semantic headers) during conversion, which could disadvantage individuals with disabilities (HTI State of AI Governance, p. 48).
sections.12.1: Yes. The assessment has identified and documented the relevant overarching legal and regulatory frameworks, specifically the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) (HTI State of AI Governance, p. 40) and the Policy for the Responsible Use of AI in Government (AI Use Policy v2.0, p. 11). Since this is a general, cross-agency utility rather than an agency-specific statutory program, there is no agency-specific enabling legislation to document.
sections.12.2:
No.
- 12.2.1: No need for legal advice has been identified or sought during this assessment process. Although the tool converts third-party web content, which could theoretically raise copyright or intellectual property implications under the Copyright Act 1968 (Cth) (HTI State of AI Governance, p. 40), no formal legal advice has been sought.
- 12.2.2: Not applicable. No legal advice has been sought or stored.
sections.11.1: No. As noted in the threshold assessment (sections 1.2 and 1.3), an Accountable Official and an Accountable Use Case Owner have not yet been designated for this utility. To establish proper accountability mechanisms during the lifecycle of the AI system, the agency must designate an accountable official and an accountable use case owner in accordance with the Policy for the Responsible Use of AI in Government (AI Use Policy v2.0, p. 11). Once designated, the accountable official will be responsible for implementing the policy and maintaining a register of in-scope AI use cases, while the accountable use case owner will be responsible for the specific use case throughout its lifecycle (AI Use Policy v2.0, p. 11).
